title: glycosmis ferruginea
intro: Glycosmis ferruginea, also known as the Yellow Ginger, is a species of plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia. The medicinal properties of Glycosmis ferruginea are attributed to its essential oil, which is rich in bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. In traditional medicine, Glycosmis ferruginea is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or oil extract, which can be used to treat various ailments or as a natural remedy for maintaining overall well-being.
